source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Platform Crane

event.top_event: Structural damage to crane boom heel section
actions_taken: Crane placed out of service by crane mechanic on 7 September 2022
Battery disconnected and lock-out tag-out procedure completed
Management of Change initiated on 13 September 2022
Crane de-rated by crane engineer in accordance with API Specification 2C
High-angle kick-out readjusted by crane mechanic on 21 September 2022
Crane boom stops inspected and impact points confirmed
event.summary: On 7 September 2022, damage to the boom heel section of the SM 192A platform crane was discovered during a monthly facility inspection. The damage consisted of bent vertical lacings and uniform bends in both upper chords of the boom heel section. Investigation revealed that the high-angle boom kick-out safety device had failed to function, allowing the boom to contact the boom stops and sustain damage. The root causes were identified as: (1) inoperable safety device due to incorrect mounted position, and (2) human performance error where crane operators failed to function test the high-angle kick-out despite checking it off on pre-use inspection forms on multiple occasions. The crane was de-rated and placed out of service.
key_phrases: boom heel upper chords contacted boom stops

threats.name: High-angle boom kick-out safety device inoperable
Failure to function test safety devices during pre-use inspection
Failure to recognize existing damage during visual inspection

controls.name: High-angle boom kick-out safety device
controls.barrier_role: Prevents boom from exceeding safe angle and contacting boom stops

supporting_text: The high angle boom kick out switch was inoperable due to its mounted position
The crane mechanic had to readjust the position of the high-angle boom kick-out to initiate at 78 degrees
the high angle boom-kickout failed to function allowing the boom to make contact with the boom stops

controls.name: Anti-two block safety device
controls.barrier_role: Prevents boom from moving back excessively

supporting_text: the high-angle kick-out and the anti-two block had been checked off the pre-use inspection, but the devices were not tested

controls.name: Crane pre-use inspection procedure
controls.barrier_role: Identifies equipment defects and verifies safety device functionality before operation

supporting_text: the CO #1 checked off all the sections on the pre-use inspection prior to physically conducting the inspection
failed to physically complete the pre-use inspection which included function testing the high angle kick-out and the anti-two block
the operators checked the high angle boom kick-out section as tested on the pre-use inspection but failed to conduct the testing
the Crane Pre-Use Inspection Form AOL-SWP-180-FRM-002 section #8 high angle kick-out was checked which gave a false indication that the device was tested

controls.name: Walk-around visual examination of crane boom
controls.barrier_role: Detects visible damage to boom and support structure

supporting_text: the crane operator failed to recognize the damage to the boom heel section during the visual examination
CO #1 noticed the bends on the upper boom cords but thought the crane was made that way

CO#1 stated to the BSEE AI that he did not notice any damage to the chord during pre-use inspection and states he noticed the bends in left and right lacings during the pre-use inspection but assumed it was the way the crane was designed

controls.name: Crane mechanic on-site inspection and verification
controls.barrier_role: Detects and verifies equipment damage; places equipment out of service

supporting_text: A crane mechanic was tasked to join operators for the life capsule removal in case the crane needed mechanical ignition work
the crane mechanic verified the two bent vertical lacings as well as uniform bends in both upper chords of the boom heel section
The crane mechanic immediately placed the crane out of service, disconnected the crane battery, and completed the lock-out tag-out procedure

controls.name: Management of Change (MOC) and crane de-rating
controls.barrier_role: Formally assesses and documents equipment limitations after damage; reduces operational risk

supporting_text: On 13 September 2022, an MOC was initiated and the crane was de-rated by a crane engineer
The crane was de-rated in accordance with API Specification 2C, with an expiration date of 12 December 2022

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
